run.output:
Q6 Revenue: 1230113636.01
Execution time: ~31 ms (average of 5 runs: 32.33, 30.35, 30.44, 31.36, 31.22 ms)

profiling.notes: perf not available; profiling deferred to optimization phase

Q6.notes: Correct result (revenue 1230113636.01), but execution time ~2x slower than target (16.5 ms from GenDB benchmark)

Q6.bottleneck: Missing zone map optimization + suboptimal thread utilization + no explicit SIMD
Q6.root_cause: Query scans 59.9M rows with selective date predicate (1 year out of 7+ year range = ~14-17% selectivity), but doesn't use zone_map.idx metadata to skip non-matching blocks. Also: (1) thread cap at 48 on 64-core system leaves 16 cores idle, (2) no explicit SIMD vectorization of predicates, (3) no prefetch hints for columns in hot loop.

analysis.overall: Q6 executes correctly but ~2x slower than benchmark target. The primary issue is missing zone map optimization which could filter out ~83% of blocks without scanning data. Secondary issues: conservative thread capping, missing SIMD acceleration. With zone maps, expect 40-50% speedup. With explicit SIMD on predicates, additional 20-30% speedup possible.

performance_optimizations.description: Load lineitem.l_shipdate.zone_map.idx and filter blocks before scanning. The zone map contains min/max shipdate per block. For Q6's date predicate [1994-01-01, 1995-01-01), skip entire blocks where max_shipdate < 1994-01-01 or min_shipdate >= 1995-01-01. This is particularly effective because: (1) lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate (storage_design.json line 24), (2) data spans years 1992-1998 (7 year range), (3) Q6 queries only 1 year, (4) zone maps are pre-computed (4.7K file). Implementation: Read zone_map.idx as array of {min, max} per block (~400 blocks for 60M rows at 150K block size), iterate blocks with atomic counter in same morsel loop, skip blocks that don't overlap [8766, 9131). Expected improvement: 40-50% speedup (eliminate ~83% of block scans for typical date distribution).
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 40-50% reduction in execution time (~15-19 ms target)

performance_optimizations.description: Implement explicit SIMD evaluation of the three predicates using AVX-512 (available per lscpu output: avx512f, avx512dq, avx512cd, avx512bw, avx512vl). Replace the scalar loop (lines 137-148) with SIMD version: (1) Load 8x int32_t shipdates into __m512i, compare against [DATE_1994_01_01, DATE_1995_01_01) with _mm512_cmpge_epi32/_mm512_cmplt_epi32 to produce masks, (2) Load 8x double discounts, compare [0.05, 0.07] with _mm512_cmp_pd_mask, (3) Load 8x quantities, compare < 24.0, (4) Combine masks with _mm512_kand, (5) Use masked load/add for extendedprice*discount contribution. Compiler auto-vectorization is incomplete for complex predicates. Expected improvement: 20-30% speedup via 8x throughput on predicates (AVX-512 vs scalar) and reduced branch mispredictions.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 20-30% reduction in execution time (~22-25 ms with zone maps)

performance_optimizations.description: Remove the cap at 48 threads (line 101: 'if (num_threads > 48)') on the 64-core system. Since Q6 uses only morsel-driven work-stealing (atomic counter), there's no contention penalty for additional threads. Each thread processes ~234K rows at current 100K morsel size or ~59M/64 = 920K rows if morsel size remains fixed. Verify that NUMA effects don't degrade performance on multi-socket systems (likely single socket here based on 44MB L3 shared cache). Expected improvement: 10-20% speedup from better load balancing on idle cores.

Add explicit software prefetch to pull discount[i] and quantity[i] into L2 cache ahead of evaluation. Current code uses _mm_prefetch include (line 13) but doesn't call it. In the inner loop (line 137-148), add: _mm_prefetch(&disc_r[i+64], _MM_HINT_T1) and _mm_prefetch(&qty_r[i+64], _MM_HINT_T1) to bring future iterations into L1/L2. Given L2 cache is 32MB per core and morsel is 100K rows, prefetching can hide memory latency for double loads (8 bytes). Expected improvement: 5-10% speedup from reduced cache misses on secondary predicates.

Current morsel size 100K rows = 100K * (4 + 8 + 8 + 8) bytes = 2.8 MB of working set. For a 44MB shared L3 cache with 64 cores (or 32 cores at once), this is reasonable but could be optimized per core. Profile suggests L3 might be saturated. Try: (1) Reduce morsel to 50K rows = 1.4 MB (better fit in per-core L2 + L3), (2) or increase to 150K = 4.2 MB if L3 not saturated. Morsel-driven approach allows easy tuning without code changes—only modify line 104 constant.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 5-15% improvement depending on actual cache behavior (estimated 10% speedup to 28 ms)

summary: Q6 iteration 1 executes correctly with result 1230113636.01 matching ground truth. However, execution time ~31 ms is ~2x slower than the GenDB benchmark target of ~16.5 ms and 50% slower than DuckDB's ~20 ms. Root cause: missing zone map optimization (40-50% potential gain), combined with suboptimal threading (only 48/64 cores utilized) and missing explicit SIMD vectorization. The zone map filter is the critical lever—with l_shipdate sorted and indexed, it can eliminate ~83% of block scans for typical date range predicates. Recommended sequence: (1) Implement zone map filtering (priority 1, low risk, 40-50% gain), (2) Add explicit SIMD predicates (priority 2, medium risk, 20-30% gain), (3) Increase thread count (priority 3, low risk, 10-20% gain). Combined, these optimizations should target ~14-16 ms (GenDB benchmark level).
